reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine for human astrovirus infection, nor are any human astrovirus vaccines in late-stage clinical trials. Vaccine development, clinical trials, and regulatory approval consistently take several years at minimum. Thus, it is functionally impossible for a human astrovirus vaccine to be legitimately completely developed, approved, and then added to the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page by February 2026. The only ways this could resolve 'Yes' are if a veterinary vaccine is added to this human-focused Wikipedia table (which is unlikely), or through page vandalism/manipulation that happens to coincide with one of the resolution dates. Due to Wikipedia's moderation and the obscurity of the disease, long-lasting vandalism is highly improbable. I am placing the probabilities at the minimum allowable lower bound (0.02) for the nearest dates, rising only to 0.04 for the 6-month horizon to account for the slight cumulative probability of unpredictable Wiki edits.
